verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a sperm-specific cell-adhesion protein essential for gamete fusion. It is a single-pass transmembrane protein of the Ig-like CAM family expressed exclusively on the sperm plasma membrane during spermatogenesis and in mature sperm. The protein has an extracellular immunoglobulin domain required for binding to its oocyte receptor (JUNO) and mediating membrane fusion. Surface accessibility is restricted to the male germline lineage — the protein's own TM anchor positions it on the outer leaflet, but only in sperm cells. This tissue/cell-type restriction to a specialized reproductive lineage qualifies as contextual surface biology rather than baseline yes.
